content:
Which tissue pattern defines Macrodystrophia Lipomatosis Macrodactyly?

{{c1::lipomatosis of the nerves and disproportionate mesenchymal proliferation of digits in the involved nerve territory}}

What anatomic distribution is typical for Macrodystrophia Lipomatosis Macrodactyly?

{{c1::the second or third digit of the hand or foot unilaterally}}

Which radiographic finding pattern suggests Macrodystrophia Lipomatosis Macrodactyly?

{{c1::hypertrophy of both soft and osseous tissues, as well as lengthened, splayed, or expanded distal phalanges}}

Which CT finding pattern suggests Macrodystrophia Lipomatosis Macrodactyly?

{{c1::excessive bone growth and adipose tissue proliferation along the peripheral nerves}}

Which MRI finding pattern suggests Macrodystrophia Lipomatosis Macrodactyly?

{{c1::hypointense fibrous bands within adipose tissue, cortical thickening, and fatty infiltration of the nerves are the major findings}}
Management for symptomatic Macrodystrophia Lipomatosis Macrodactyly ranges from {{c1::orthotics}} to {{c2::surgical debulking or osteotomy for patients with significant functional limitations}}.

Which mechanisms are proposed causes of Macrodystrophia Lipomatosis Macrodactyly?

{{c1::Lipomatous degeneration, intrauterine segmentation anomalies, and somatic activating PIK3CA mutations}}

What diagnosis is suggested by this radiographic appearance?

{{c1::Macrodystrophia Lipomatosis Macrodactyly}}
